title: 40-minute caregiver routine for grandma — Aug 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • A forty-minute caregiver routine Yahir wrote for himself (or someone else) covering grandma's morning routine. Modified the next day — actively in use.
  • Hyper-specific operational detail:
    • Right-handed spoons fork. Small bite-size. Fridge: left door, then right, do not slam.
    • Coffee: 2 scoops; leave so she can do it; after, fill water up to top line.
    • Yogurt with strawberry: 1 scoop granola, 1 dollop yogurt; mix; give to grandma.
    • Strawberry prep: cut tops off, cube horizontal then vertical, 2 big strawberries.
    • Orange juice: ask which cup; save little OJ bottle with straw.
    • Bacon: 2 strips, 30-min timer.
    • Eggs: pre-hard-boiled, 8-part cut, half.
    • Salad: 2/3 of bowl, 1.5" cucumber, sprinkle carrots + lettuce, slice 3 baby tomatoes; shrink-wrap.
    • Soup: ask which kind, split 2 bowls + shrink-wrap, toast at 2-min mark.
    • Dog food: 2 oz.
    • Coffee filter swap-out, water replace.

Key signal

"Right-handed spoons fork. Small bite-size. Fridge left door then right, do not slam."

Why it matters in the graph

  • This is the unspoken bridge income Yahir doesn't fully foreground in Memory or 2025 09 23 how im scraping by — the caregiver work for grandma that has time-cost implications layered on top of the gym-job overnight shifts.
  • Pairs with 2026 01 04 shef instruction (the freezer-stocking job) and the 2025 08 21 austin prep move-out logistics — Yahir was juggling multiple manual jobs through summer/fall 2025.
  • The document-the-routine-for-handover form is itself a Yahir-typical move (see 2025 09 08 logging health data naviwell — same instinct applied to clinic workflow). He thinks in runbooks.
  • "Don't slam the fridge" → tone of intimate caregiver knowledge — care-load + emotional load both visible in one note.

Open questions

  • Which grandma — paternal or maternal? (Mom's mom is in 2025 03 15 than usual recently — "my mom still birthing her dad," suggesting maternal grandparents are alive.)
  • Was this routine handed off, or kept by Yahir himself?
